dataset: GNU Health 5.0 demo database (gnuhealth-50-demo.sql from gnuhealth.org)

pi_items.source: Design choice. Live DB query confirmed no pre-existing 'Marcus Torres' in party_party (evidence: 07_patient_absence.log). Agent must register this new patient.
Design choice. Live DB query confirmed no pre-existing 'Torres' in party_party. Patient must be created by the agent.
Design choice. Live DB query confirmed no pre-existing BLL_PANEL in gnuhealth_lab_test_type (evidence: 04_lab_test_types.log shows empty result). Agent must create this lab test type.
Design choice for the lab test type name. No pre-existing record. Agent creates it.

Web search confirmed: BLL (Blood Lead Level), ZPP (Zinc Protoporphyrin), FEP (Free Erythrocyte Protoporphyrin) are universally standard abbreviations used by CDC, OSHA, NIOSH. Sources: CDC NIOSH BLL Reference, OSHA Lead Standards 1910.1025 Appendix C.

Live DB verification (evidence: 03_icd10_codes.log): T56.0 (id=9506, 'Toxic effect: Lead and its compounds'), D64.9 (id=1845, 'Anaemia, unspecified'), I10 (id=3676, 'Essential (primary) hypertension'). All confirmed present in gnuhealth_pathology table. ICD-10-CM codes cross-verified via icd10data.com.

Live DB verification (evidence: 03_icd10_codes.log): I25.1 (id=3727, 'Atherosclerotic heart disease') and E11 (id=2012, 'Type 2 diabetes mellitus') both confirmed present in gnuhealth_pathology. ICD-10-CM codes cross-verified via icd10data.com.

Standard drug name. IMPORTANT SCHEMA QUIRK: In GNU Health 5.0, allergies are NOT stored in a separate gnuhealth_patient_allergy table. They are stored in gnuhealth_patient_disease with the is_allergy=true flag. Live schema verification confirmed columns: is_allergy (boolean), allergy_type, disease_severity all exist on gnuhealth_patient_disease (evidence: 12_schema_verification.log).

Live DB verification (evidence: 05_medications.log, 05b_medication_details.log): 'succimer' exists as medicament id=43; 'Ferrous Sulfate' exists as medicament id=452. DMSA is an alias for Succimer (dimercaptosuccinic acid). Web search confirmed Succimer is FDA-approved chelation for lead poisoning. Ferrous Sulfate is standard iron supplementation for lead-related anemia.

Live DB verification (evidence: 04_lab_test_types.log): CBC (id=2, 'COMPLETE BLOOD COUNT') and CMP (id=28, 'COMPREHENSIVE METABOLIC PANEL') both confirmed present in gnuhealth_lab_test_type. BLL_PANEL does not pre-exist (agent creates it). NOTE: Lab test types require a product_id (NOT NULL FK to product_product) — setup_task.sh creates service products for CBC and CMP if they don't exist.

Design choice for vitals the agent should enter. Clinically plausible: lead exposure causes hypertension. DB column confirmed as 'systolic' (integer) in gnuhealth_patient_evaluation (evidence: 12_schema_verification.log).
Design choice. DB column confirmed as 'diastolic' (integer) in gnuhealth_patient_evaluation (evidence: 12_schema_verification.log).

Design choice. CRITICAL SCHEMA QUIRK: The metadata key says 'eval_heart_rate' but the actual DB column in gnuhealth_patient_evaluation is 'bpm' (NOT 'heart_rate'). Live schema verification confirmed this (evidence: 12_schema_verification.log shows 'gnuhealth_patient_evaluation.bpm: OK'). The export_result.sh correctly queries 'bpm'. The task description says 'HR 78' which the agent enters into the 'Heart Rate' or 'BPM' field in the GUI.
Design choice for lenient verification window. Task description says '14 days'; metadata allows 7-21 day window for partial credit. This is consistent with clinical follow-up flexibility.
Design choice for lenient verification window (7-21 days around the 14-day target).
Descriptive label for the clinical role performing this task. Not a data claim requiring verification.
Descriptive label for the industry context. Not a data claim requiring verification.

privileged_info_summary: This task requires the agent to perform a complete occupational health intake in GNU Health 5.0 (Tryton Sao web client). The agent must create a new patient (Marcus Torres, DOB 1985-06-14, Male) who does NOT pre-exist in the demo database, and a new lab test type (BLL_PANEL) that also does NOT pre-exist. Key ground truth for verification: (1) Lab test type: code BLL_PANEL with 3 analytes — BLL (Blood Lead Level, ref <5 ug/dL), ZPP (Zinc Protoporphyrin, ref <40 ug/dL), FEP (Free Erythrocyte Protoporphyrin, ref <35 ug/dL). (2) Patient: Marcus Torres registered with DOB 1985-06-14, Male. (3) Three ICD-10 diagnoses: T56.0 (lead toxicity, confirmed in DB as id=9506), D64.9 (anemia, id=1845), I10 (hypertension, id=3676). (4) Allergy to Penicillin — IMPORTANT: in GNU Health 5.0, allergies are stored in the gnuhealth_patient_disease table with is_allergy=true flag, NOT in a separate allergy table. (5) Family history: father with I25.1 (id=3727, atherosclerotic heart disease, deceased age 62), mother with E11 (id=2012, type 2 diabetes, alive). The family disease table is named gnuhealth_patient_family_diseases (plural). (6) Health evaluation with vitals: systolic=145, diastolic=92, bpm=78 (NOTE: the DB column is 'bpm' not 'heart_rate'), temperature=37.0. Chief complaint: chronic fatigue, headaches, abdominal pain for 3 weeks. (7) Three lab orders: BLL_PANEL (custom, agent-created), CBC (pre-exists as id=2), CMP (pre-exists as id=28). (8) Two prescriptions: Succimer/DMSA (medicament id=43) and Ferrous Sulfate (medicament id=452). Prescription lines are in gnuhealth_prescription_line (FK presc_order), and medicament FK to product is 'product' not 'name'. (9) Follow-up appointment in 14 days (acceptable window: 7-21 days). (10) Contamination/decoy: Ana Isabel Betz already has a T56.0 diagnosis — this was injected as an anti-cheat trap. The agent must create records for Marcus Torres specifically, not modify Ana Betz. Login credentials: admin / gnusolidario.
